what_happened: None of Maui's 80 outdoor warning sirens were activated as wildfire overran Lahaina; emergency chief defended the decision then resigned
Only 24% of county residents had opted in to Everbridge alerts; as few as 1 in 5 people sent evacuation warnings confirmed receiving them
Opt-in CodeRED alerts reached about 7;000 of 52;000 evacuees; fewer than half of residents had signed up and 17 cell towers burned the first day
County's Everbridge system never sent an evacuation alert to Talent before the Almeda Fire burned through; many residents got no official warning
Kerr County had no outdoor flood sirens along the Guadalupe despite years of debate; overnight cellphone alerts couldn't reach campers without phones; 100+ died
Evacuation orders for west Altadena were not issued until 3:25 a.m.; over 9 hours after ignition; 18 of the fire's 19 deaths occurred west of Lake Avenue
Lee County ordered evacuation a day after neighboring counties and under 24 hours before landfall; 72 of Florida's 149 Ian deaths were in Lee County
Errant ballistic missile alert went to phones statewide during a drill; correction took 38 minutes because no cancellation process existed
Officials chose not to send WEA cellphone alerts as the Tubbs Fire raced into Santa Rosa overnight; state review cited limited awareness of the system; 24 died
Requested IPAWS evacuation message was never delivered as phones internet and power failed; the alert reached only radio and TV; 14 died
Debris flow killed 23; hardest-hit low-lying neighborhoods were under voluntary warning rather than mandatory evacuation order and many residents stayed
County after-action review found outdated policies inconsistent practices and communications vulnerabilities weakened Woolsey Fire alerts and evacuations
Hacker triggered all 156 outdoor warning sirens for about 90 minutes near midnight; more than 4;400 calls flooded 911 and the system was shut down
Residents said they heard no tornado sirens as the EF4 leveled much of town; a local siren was reportedly not working when the storm hit
City lost power at the moment staff went to activate outdoor sirens so they never sounded; EF3 struck with minimal warning killing three
Sirens never sounded for the May 16 EF3; CEMA staff were offsite and the fire department never pressed the button; report cited multiple cascading failures
Feared emergency spillway collapse prompted a sudden evacuation order for about 188;000 people downstream; residents fled with little notice jamming roads
Edenville and Sanford dam failures forced rapid evacuation of about 11;000 residents; FEMA conducted a review of the dam incident response and alerting
Spencer Dam collapsed during the 2019 bomb cyclone; the man living below was swept away and his family said he was not given enough time to evacuate
Homes behind Addicks and Barker dams were left out of evacuation orders when floodwater releases began; officials knew releases would flood neighborhoods
Historic rains breached dozens of dams statewide; Gills Creek watershed dams failed before dawn forcing sudden evacuations around Columbia; 19 died
Flash Flood Emergency was issued only as record floodwater was already sweeping through town; 20 died and the 911 center itself was flooded
Flash Flood Warning came at 4:26 p.m. and Flash Flood Emergency at 4:40 p.m. as Main Street hit its highest water; second catastrophic flood in two years
Ida's record 3.5 in/hr rain triggered NYC's first Flash Flood Emergency; most of the city's deaths were drownings in unregulated basement apartments
Downed cell towers delayed transmission of Buncombe County's evacuation order; 29 of 43 NC counties where Helene deaths occurred sent no IPAWS alerts
Six Impact Plastics workers died after staying at the plant as Helene floodwaters rose; survivors allege employees were not released in time to escape
Missing evacuation polygon caused a Kenneth Fire alert to hit nearly 10 million phones countywide; a second erroneous alert followed the next day
285 Mexico Beach residents defied mandatory evacuation before Category 5 landfall; 80% of surveyed residents underestimated the storm; 14-ft surge hit
Ida intensified too fast for New Orleans to order mandatory evacuation or contraflow; the city needs 72 hours to fully evacuate and had far less
Blanco River rose over 40 feet in the middle of the night; the area's lone river gauge was overwhelmed then destroyed limiting warning; at least 12 died
Sheriff issued 59 CodeRED notifications but opt-in alerts reached only 31;979 registered phones; four of the seven dead were killed in their own neighborhoods
Level 3 evacuation orders for Santiam Canyon towns were issued suddenly near midnight as fires merged; the late orders sowed confusion and chaos
Evacuation warnings were late; incomplete or nonexistent; orders relied on landlines many residents no longer had; some fled only on seeing flames
Broadcasters urged residents to flee south from the El Reno tornado; mass car evacuation gridlocked freeways; 500+ could have died had it held its track
Sudden gas-system overpressure fires forced about 30;000 people to evacuate three towns at once; notification was split across state WEA and separate town systems
Most residents learned of the overnight flood by watching water rise; NWS alerts often failed to arrive amid poor cell and broadband coverage; 39+ died
Candle factory workers said supervisors threatened firing if they left ahead of the tornado; the plant collapsed and eight workers died
EF3 hit a city with no outdoor tornado sirens anywhere in Otsego County; CodeRED phone alerts went out eight minutes before touchdown; two died
Thousand-year flood killed 23; a 2004 statewide flood protection plan recommending warning system upgrades had never been implemented
More than 800 homes were ordered evacuated; the sheriff said the canyon's single route in and out created safety concerns during the state's largest fire
Evacuation gridlock led drivers to abandon nearly 200 cars on Palisades Drive and Sunset Blvd; bulldozers pushed vehicles aside to clear escape routes
Statewide WEA reading only 'civil emergency' was sent for a local drinking-water advisory; the cryptic alert caused panic well beyond Salem
Landslide killed 43 with no warning; decades of documented slide hazard were never sufficiently communicated to residents below the hillside
Tsunami warning evacuation gridlocked Oahu roads for hours with multihour commutes; officials cited over-evacuation and gaps in evacuation planning
EF3 collapsed an Amazon warehouse; the storm shelter location was not properly communicated and six workers sheltering in a bathroom died
County debuted Zonehaven evacuation zones during the CZU fire and retained the zone system for debris-flow evacuations in the burn scar
Cal Fire and Zonehaven officials said zone-based evacuations were carried out in a timely manner limiting injuries and deaths during the Caldor Fire
City directed residents to Genasys Protect zone maps for real-time Franklin Fire evacuation status and phased zone-by-zone repopulation
Emergency teams issued 400+ zone status changes via Genasys Protect in the first two weeks of the January fires; hundreds of thousands used the app
